revision.text:
王墓山古墳 (倉敷市)
[[Category:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市)]][[ja:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市)]]

{{Ancient_Kofun_in_Japan |Name=Ōbosan Kofun |Image=[[File:Obosan Kofun (Kurashiki), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (sarcophagus visible at the far right) |Alternative_Name=Akai Nishi No.1 Kofun |Location={{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}{{ill|Kurashiki|ja|倉敷市}}, Nihata (Ōbo-no-Oka Historic Park) |Coordinates={{Coord|34|39|35.10|N|133|49|35.34|E}} |Shape=Circular or square-shaped mound |Scale=Diameter/side length: 25m |Burial_Facility={{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室}} with a constructed house-shaped sarcophagus |Construction_Period=Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} |Artifacts=Numerous grave goods |Cultural_Property={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}, designated in 1959 |Image2=[[File:Obosan Kofun (Kurashiki), sekkan.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped sarcophagus}}]] }} '''Ōbosan Kofun''' (王墓山古墳) is a burial mound located in {{ill|Kurashiki|ja|倉敷市}}, Nihata, Okayama Prefecture. It is part of the Ōbosan Kofun Cluster (or Akai Nishi Kofun Cluster) and is designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture. == Overview == Ōbosan Kofun is situated on a low hill along the right bank of the Ashimori River. This area is known for its concentration of burial mounds, with Ōbosan Kofun serving as the primary tumulus of the cluster. The mound and burial chamber underwent significant alterations during the late Meiji period. The original shape of the mound is estimated to have been either circular or square, with a diameter or side length of approximately 25 meters. The burial chamber was likely a horizontal stone chamber, although no trace of it remains today due to stone extraction. The site retains a house-shaped sarcophagus placed near the mound. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 6th century. The sarcophagus exhibits close similarities to that of the {{ill|Kōmorizuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, indicating a potential connection between the two sites. Ōbosan Kofun was designated a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture in 1959. Today, it is part of the Ōbo-no-Oka Historic Park and is open to the public. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is presumed to have been a horizontal stone chamber. However, the chamber was destroyed during the late Meiji period for stone quarrying, and only the sarcophagus remains. The sarcophagus, crafted from shell limestone sourced from {{ill|Ibara, Okayama|ja|井原市}}, is a constructed house-shaped type comprising seven stone slabs. Its dimensions are as follows: * Sarcophagus body: 2.05 meters in length * Sarcophagus material: Shell limestone (Namakata stone) == Artifacts == Grave goods unearthed from the site include: * A bronze mirror (decorated with Buddhist and animal motifs) * Gilded bronze horse trappings * Iron weapons * Ornaments * Pottery (Sue ware) These artifacts are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. However, it is suggested that some of the items may originate from neighboring burial mounds within the cluster. File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 出土 画文帯仏獣鏡.JPG|Bronze mirror
{{small|Exhibited at Tokyo National Museum}} File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 竪矧広板鋲留衝角付冑.JPG|Iron helmet with rivets
{{small|Tokyo National Museum}} File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 出土 鐘形杏葉.JPG|Bell-shaped horse gear
{{small|Tokyo National Museum}} File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 広口壺 (J-7646).JPG|Sue ware jar
{{small|Tokyo National Museum}} File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 出土 須恵器 蓋付・須恵器 高台付椀.JPG|Sue ware lidded vessels and high-footed bowls
{{small|Tokyo National Museum}} == Cultural Properties == === Okayama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site * Ōbosan Kofun - Designated on March 27, 1959[http://www.pref.okayama.jp/cgi-bin/bunka/culture/controller/client/resultDetail.cgi?id=336 Ōbosan Kofun] (Okayama Prefecture “Cultural Properties of Okayama”).. == Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Tatezuka Mound|ja|楯築墳丘墓}} - One of the largest {{ill|Yayoi|ja|弥生時代}} burial mounds in Japan. * Nihata Temple Site - Ruins of an ancient temple. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Explanatory panel (Kurashiki City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1974|chapter=Ōbosan Kofun (Akai Nishi No.1)|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/13111|title=Ōbosan Archaeological Sites|publisher=Kurashiki City Board of Education|pages=187–198}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Ōbosan Kofun|title=Okayama Prefecture Place Names|series=[[Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia]] Vol. 34|publisher=Heibonsha}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Obosan Kofun (Kurashiki)}} * [http://www.pref.okayama.jp/cgi-bin/bunka/culture/controller/client/resultDetail.cgi?id=336 Ōbosan Kofun] - Okayama Prefecture “Cultural Properties of Okayama” * [http://www.city.kurashiki.okayama.jp/5510.htm Ōbosan Kofun] - Kurashiki City Official Site [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kurashiki]] [[Category:Circular tumuli]] [[Category:Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]] [[ja:王墓山古墳]]

However, it is suggested that some of the items may originate from neighboring burial mounds within the cluster. File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 出土 画文帯仏獣鏡.JPG|Bronze mirror

It is part of the Ōbosan Kofun Cluster (or Akai Nishi Kofun Cluster) and is designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture. == Overview == Ōbosan Kofun is situated on a low hill along the right bank of the Ashimori River. This area is known for its concentration of burial mounds, with Ōbosan Kofun serving as the primary tumulus of the cluster. The mound and burial chamber underwent significant alterations during the late Meiji period. The original shape of the mound is estimated to have been either circular or square, with a diameter or side length of approximately 25 meters. The burial chamber was likely a horizontal stone chamber, although no trace of it remains today due to stone extraction. The site retains a house-shaped sarcophagus placed near the mound. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 6th century. The sarcophagus exhibits close similarities to that of the {{ill|Kōmorizuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, indicating a potential connection between the two sites. Ōbosan Kofun was designated a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture in 1959. Today, it is part of the Ōbo-no-Oka Historic Park and is open to the public. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is presumed to have been a horizontal stone chamber. However, the chamber was destroyed during the late Meiji period for stone quarrying, and only the sarcophagus remains. The sarcophagus, crafted from shell limestone sourced from {{ill|Ibara, Okayama|ja|井原市}}, is a constructed house-shaped type comprising seven stone slabs. Its dimensions are as follows: * Sarcophagus body: 2.05 meters in length * Sarcophagus material: Shell limestone (Namakata stone) == Artifacts == Grave goods unearthed from the site include: * A bronze mirror (decorated with Buddhist and animal motifs) * Gilded bronze horse trappings * Iron weapons * Ornaments * Pottery (Sue ware) These artifacts are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. {{small|Tokyo National Museum}} == Cultural Properties == === Okayama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site * Ōbosan Kofun - Designated on March 27, 1959[http://www.pref.okayama.jp/cgi-bin/bunka/culture/controller/client/resultDetail.cgi?id=336 Ōbosan Kofun] (Okayama Prefecture “Cultural Properties of Okayama”).. == Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Tatetsuki Site|ja|楯築墳丘墓}} - One of the largest {{ill|Yayoi|ja|弥生時代}} burial mounds in Japan. * Nihata Temple Site - Ruins of an ancient temple. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Explanatory panel (Kurashiki City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1974|chapter=Ōbosan Kofun (Akai Nishi No.1)|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/13111|title=Ōbosan Archaeological Sites|publisher=Kurashiki City Board of Education|pages=187–198}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Ōbosan Kofun|title=Okayama Prefecture Place Names|series=[[Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia]] Vol. 34|publisher=Heibonsha}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Obosan Kofun (Kurashiki)}} * [http://www.pref.okayama.jp/cgi-bin/bunka/culture/controller/client/resultDetail.cgi?id=336 Ōbosan Kofun] - Okayama Prefecture “Cultural Properties of Okayama” * [http://www.city.kurashiki.okayama.jp/5510.htm Ōbosan Kofun] - Kurashiki City Official Site [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kurashiki]] [[Category:Circular tumuli]] [[Category:Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōbosan Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōbosan Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōbosan Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:pages to try to fix ills]] [[ja:王墓山古墳]]

{{Ancient_Kofun_in_Japan |Name=Ōbosan Kofun |image=[[File:Obosan Kofun (Kurashiki), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (sarcophagus visible at the far right) |Alternative_Name=Akai Nishi No.1 Kofun |location={{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}{{ill|Kurashiki|ja|倉敷市}}, Nihata (Ōbo-no-Oka Historic Park) |coordinates={{Coord|34|39|35.10|N|133|49|35.34|E}} |type=Circular or square-shaped mound |Scale=Diameter/side length: 25m |Burial_Facility={{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室}} with a constructed house-shaped sarcophagus |Construction_Period=Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} |Artifacts=Numerous grave goods |Cultural_Property={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}, designated in 1959 |image2=[[File:Obosan Kofun (Kurashiki), sekkan.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped sarcophagus}}]] }} '''Ōbosan Kofun''' (王墓山古墳) is a burial mound located in {{ill|Kurashiki|ja|倉敷市}}, Nihata, Okayama Prefecture. It is part of the Ōbosan Kofun Cluster (or Akai Nishi Kofun Cluster) and is designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture. == Overview == Ōbosan Kofun is situated on a low hill along the right bank of the Ashimori River. This area is known for its concentration of burial mounds, with Ōbosan Kofun serving as the primary tumulus of the cluster. The mound and burial chamber underwent significant alterations during the late Meiji period. The original shape of the mound is estimated to have been either circular or square, with a diameter or side length of approximately 25 meters. The burial chamber was likely a horizontal stone chamber, although no trace of it remains today due to stone extraction. The site retains a house-shaped sarcophagus placed near the mound. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 6th century. The sarcophagus exhibits close similarities to that of the {{ill|Kōmorizuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, indicating a potential connection between the two sites. Ōbosan Kofun was designated a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture in 1959. Today, it is part of the Ōbo-no-Oka Historic Park and is open to the public. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is presumed to have been a horizontal stone chamber. However, the chamber was destroyed during the late Meiji period for stone quarrying, and only the sarcophagus remains. The sarcophagus, crafted from shell limestone sourced from {{ill|Ibara, Okayama|ja|井原市}}, is a constructed house-shaped type comprising seven stone slabs. Its dimensions are as follows: * Sarcophagus body: 2.05 meters in length * Sarcophagus material: Shell limestone (Namakata stone) == Artifacts == Grave goods unearthed from the site include: * A bronze mirror (decorated with Buddhist and animal motifs) * Gilded bronze horse trappings * Iron weapons * Ornaments * Pottery (Sue ware) These artifacts are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. However, it is suggested that some of the items may originate from neighboring burial mounds within the cluster. File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 出土 画文帯仏獣鏡.JPG|Bronze mirror
{{small|Exhibited at Tokyo National Museum}} File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 竪矧広板鋲留衝角付冑.JPG|Iron helmet with rivets
{{small|Tokyo National Museum}} File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 出土 鐘形杏葉.JPG|Bell-shaped horse gear
{{small|Tokyo National Museum}} File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 広口壺 (J-7646).JPG|Sue ware jar
{{small|Tokyo National Museum}} File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 出土 須恵器 蓋付・須恵器 高台付椀.JPG|Sue ware lidded vessels and high-footed bowls

revision.text:
{{Ancient_Kofun_in_Japan |Name=Ōbosan Kofun |image=[[File:Obosan Kofun (Kurashiki), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (sarcophagus visible at the far right) |Alternative_Name=Akai Nishi No.1 Kofun |location={{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}{{ill|Kurashiki|ja|倉敷市}}, Nihata (Ōbo-no-Oka Historic Park) |coordinates={{Coord|34|39|35.10|N|133|49|35.34|E}} |type=Circular or square-shaped mound |Scale=Diameter/side length: 25m |Burial_Facility={{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室}} with a constructed house-shaped sarcophagus |Construction_Period=Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} |Artifacts=Numerous grave goods |Cultural_Property={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}, designated in 1959 |image2=[[File:Obosan Kofun (Kurashiki), sekkan.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped sarcophagus}}]] }} '''Ōbosan Kofun''' (王墓山古墳) is a burial mound located in {{ill|Kurashiki|ja|倉敷市}}, Nihata, Okayama Prefecture. It is part of the Ōbosan Kofun Cluster (or Akai Nishi Kofun Cluster) and is designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture. == Overview == Ōbosan Kofun is situated on a low hill along the right bank of the Ashimori River. This area is known for its concentration of burial mounds, with Ōbosan Kofun serving as the primary tumulus of the cluster. The mound and burial chamber underwent significant alterations during the late Meiji period. The original shape of the mound is estimated to have been either circular or square, with a diameter or side length of approximately 25 meters. The burial chamber was likely a horizontal stone chamber, although no trace of it remains today due to stone extraction. The site retains a house-shaped sarcophagus placed near the mound. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 6th century. The sarcophagus exhibits close similarities to that of the {{ill|Kōmorizuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, indicating a potential connection between the two sites. Ōbosan Kofun was designated a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture in 1959. Today, it is part of the Ōbo-no-Oka Historic Park and is open to the public. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is presumed to have been a horizontal stone chamber. However, the chamber was destroyed during the late Meiji period for stone quarrying, and only the sarcophagus remains. The sarcophagus, crafted from shell limestone sourced from {{ill|Ibara, Okayama|ja|井原市}}, is a constructed house-shaped type comprising seven stone slabs. Its dimensions are as follows: * Sarcophagus body: 2.05 meters in length * Sarcophagus material: Shell limestone (Namakata stone) == Artifacts == Grave goods unearthed from the site include: * A bronze mirror (decorated with Buddhist and animal motifs) * Gilded bronze horse trappings * Iron weapons * Ornaments * Pottery (Sue ware) These artifacts are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. However, it is suggested that some of the items may originate from neighboring burial mounds within the cluster. File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 出土 画文帯仏獣鏡.JPG|Bronze mirror
{{small|Exhibited at Tokyo National Museum}} File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 竪矧広板鋲留衝角付冑.JPG|Iron helmet with rivets
{{small|Tokyo National Museum}} File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 出土 鐘形杏葉.JPG|Bell-shaped horse gear
{{small|Tokyo National Museum}} File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 広口壺 (J-7646).JPG|Sue ware jar
{{small|Tokyo National Museum}} File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 出土 須恵器 蓋付・須恵器 高台付椀.JPG|Sue ware lidded vessels and high-footed bowls

revision.text:
{{Ancient_Kofun_in_Japan |Name=Ōbosan Kofun |image=[[File:Obosan Kofun (Kurashiki), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (sarcophagus visible at the far right) |Alternative_Name=Akai Nishi No.1 Kofun |location={{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}{{ill|Kurashiki|ja|倉敷市}}, Nihata (Ōbo-no-Oka Historic Park) |coordinates={{Coord|34|39|35.10|N|133|49|35.34|E}} |type=Circular or square-shaped mound |Scale=Diameter/side length: 25m |Burial_Facility={{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室}} with a constructed house-shaped sarcophagus |Construction_Period=Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} |Artifacts=Numerous grave goods |Cultural_Property={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}, designated in 1959 |image2=[[File:Obosan Kofun (Kurashiki), sekkan.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped sarcophagus}} }} '''Ōbosan Kofun''' (王墓山古墳) is a burial mound located in {{ill|Kurashiki|ja|倉敷市}}, Nihata, Okayama Prefecture. It is part of the Ōbosan Kofun Cluster (or Akai Nishi Kofun Cluster) and is designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture. == Overview == Ōbosan Kofun is situated on a low hill along the right bank of the Ashimori River. This area is known for its concentration of burial mounds, with Ōbosan Kofun serving as the primary tumulus of the cluster. The mound and burial chamber underwent significant alterations during the late Meiji period. The original shape of the mound is estimated to have been either circular or square, with a diameter or side length of approximately 25 meters. The burial chamber was likely a horizontal stone chamber, although no trace of it remains today due to stone extraction. The site retains a house-shaped sarcophagus placed near the mound. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 6th century. The sarcophagus exhibits close similarities to that of the {{ill|Kōmorizuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, indicating a potential connection between the two sites. Ōbosan Kofun was designated a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture in 1959. Today, it is part of the Ōbo-no-Oka Historic Park and is open to the public. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is presumed to have been a horizontal stone chamber. However, the chamber was destroyed during the late Meiji period for stone quarrying, and only the sarcophagus remains. The sarcophagus, crafted from shell limestone sourced from {{ill|Ibara, Okayama|ja|井原市}}, is a constructed house-shaped type comprising seven stone slabs. Its dimensions are as follows: * Sarcophagus body: 2.05 meters in length * Sarcophagus material: Shell limestone (Namakata stone) == Artifacts == Grave goods unearthed from the site include: * A bronze mirror (decorated with Buddhist and animal motifs) * Gilded bronze horse trappings * Iron weapons * Ornaments * Pottery (Sue ware) These artifacts are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. However, it is suggested that some of the items may originate from neighboring burial mounds within the cluster. File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 出土 画文帯仏獣鏡.JPG|Bronze mirror
{{small|Exhibited at Tokyo National Museum}} File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 竪矧広板鋲留衝角付冑.JPG|Iron helmet with rivets
{{small|Tokyo National Museum}} File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 出土 鐘形杏葉.JPG|Bell-shaped horse gear
{{small|Tokyo National Museum}} File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 広口壺 (J-7646).JPG|Sue ware jar
{{small|Tokyo National Museum}} File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 出土 須恵器 蓋付・須恵器 高台付椀.JPG|Sue ware lidded vessels and high-footed bowls
{{small|Tokyo National Museum}} == Cultural Properties == === Okayama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Ōbosan Kofun - Designated on March 27, 1959[http://www.pref.okayama.jp/cgi-bin/bunka/culture/controller/client/resultDetail.cgi?id=336 Ōbosan Kofun] (Okayama Prefecture “Cultural Properties of Okayama”).. == Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Tatetsuki Site|ja|楯築墳丘墓}} - One of the largest {{ill|Yayoi|ja|弥生時代}} burial mounds in Japan. * Nihata Temple Site - Ruins of an ancient temple. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Explanatory panel (Kurashiki City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1974|chapter=Ōbosan Kofun (Akai Nishi No.1)|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/13111|title=Ōbosan Archaeological Sites|publisher=Kurashiki City Board of Education|pages=187–198}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Ōbosan Kofun|title=Okayama Prefecture Place Names|series=[[Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia]] Vol. 34|publisher=Heibonsha}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Obosan Kofun (Kurashiki)}} * [http://www.pref.okayama.jp/cgi-bin/bunka/culture/controller/client/resultDetail.cgi?id=336 Ōbosan Kofun] - Okayama Prefecture “Cultural Properties of Okayama” * [http://www.city.kurashiki.okayama.jp/5510.htm Ōbosan Kofun] - Kurashiki City Official Site [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kurashiki]] [[Category:Circular tumuli]] [[Category:Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:long o change]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Obosan Kofun}}[[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Obosan Kofun}}[[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:long o change]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Obosan Kofun}}[[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Obosan Kofun}}[[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:long o change]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Obosan Kofun}}[[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Obosan Kofun}}[[ja:王墓山古墳]]

{{small|Tokyo National Museum}} == Cultural Properties == === Okayama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Ōbosan Kofun - Designated on March 27, 1959[http://www.pref.okayama.jp/cgi-bin/bunka/culture/controller/client/resultDetail.cgi?id=336 Ōbosan Kofun] (Okayama Prefecture “Cultural Properties of Okayama”).. == Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Tatetsuki Site|ja|楯築墳丘墓}} - One of the largest {{ill|Yayoi|ja|弥生時代}} burial mounds in Japan. * Nihata Temple Site - Ruins of an ancient temple. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Explanatory panel (Kurashiki City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1974|chapter=Ōbosan Kofun (Akai Nishi No.1)|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/13111|title=Ōbosan Archaeological Sites|publisher=Kurashiki City Board of Education|pages=187–198}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Ōbosan Kofun|title=Okayama Prefecture Place Names|series=[[Japanese Historical Place Names Encyclopedia]] Vol. 34|publisher=Heibonsha}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Obosan Kofun (Kurashiki)}} * [http://www.pref.okayama.jp/cgi-bin/bunka/culture/controller/client/resultDetail.cgi?id=336 Ōbosan Kofun] - Okayama Prefecture “Cultural Properties of Okayama” * [http://www.city.kurashiki.okayama.jp/5510.htm Ōbosan Kofun] - Kurashiki City Official Site [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kurashiki]] [[Category:Circular tumuli]] [[Category:Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:long o change]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Obosan Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Obosan Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:long o change]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Obosan Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Obosan Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:long o change]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Obosan Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Obosan Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[ja:王墓山古墳]]

{{Ancient_Kofun_in_Japan |name=Ōbosan Kofun |image=[[File:Obosan Kofun (Kurashiki), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (sarcophagus visible at the far right) |Alternative_name=Akai Nishi No.1 Kofun |location={{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}{{ill|Kurashiki|ja|倉敷市}}, Nihata (Ōbo-no-Oka Historic Park) |coordinates={{Coord|34|39|35.10|N|133|49|35.34|E}} |type=Circular or square-shaped mound |Scale=Diameter/side length: 25m |Burial_Facility={{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室}} with a constructed house-shaped sarcophagus |Construction_Period=Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} |Artifacts=Numerous grave goods |Cultural_Property={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}, designated in 1959 |image2=[[File:Obosan Kofun (Kurashiki), sekkan.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped sarcophagus}}]] }} '''Ōbosan Kofun''' (王墓山古墳) is a burial mound located in {{ill|Kurashiki|ja|倉敷市}}, Nihata, Okayama Prefecture. It is part of the Ōbosan Kofun Cluster (or Akai Nishi Kofun Cluster) and is designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture. == Overview == Ōbosan Kofun is situated on a low hill along the right bank of the Ashimori River. This area is known for its concentration of burial mounds, with Ōbosan Kofun serving as the primary tumulus of the cluster. The mound and burial chamber underwent significant alterations during the late Meiji period. The original shape of the mound is estimated to have been either circular or square, with a diameter or side length of approximately 25 meters. The burial chamber was likely a horizontal stone chamber, although no trace of it remains today due to stone extraction. The site retains a house-shaped sarcophagus placed near the mound. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 6th century. The sarcophagus exhibits close similarities to that of the {{ill|Kōmorizuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, indicating a potential connection between the two sites. Ōbosan Kofun was designated a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture in 1959. Today, it is part of the Ōbo-no-Oka Historic Park and is open to the public. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is presumed to have been a horizontal stone chamber. However, the chamber was destroyed during the late Meiji period for stone quarrying, and only the sarcophagus remains. The sarcophagus, crafted from shell limestone sourced from {{ill|Ibara, Okayama|ja|井原市}}, is a constructed house-shaped type comprising seven stone slabs. Its dimensions are as follows: * Sarcophagus body: 2.05 meters in length * Sarcophagus material: Shell limestone (Namakata stone) == Artifacts == Grave goods unearthed from the site include: * A bronze mirror (decorated with Buddhist and animal motifs) * Gilded bronze horse trappings * Iron weapons * Ornaments * Pottery (Sue ware) These artifacts are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. However, it is suggested that some of the items may originate from neighboring burial mounds within the cluster. File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 出土 画文帯仏獣鏡.JPG|Bronze mirror
{{small|Exhibited at Tokyo National Museum}} File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 竪矧広板鋲留衝角付冑.JPG|Iron helmet with rivets
{{small|Tokyo National Museum}} File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 出土 鐘形杏葉.JPG|Bell-shaped horse gear
{{small|Tokyo National Museum}} File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 広口壺 (J-7646).JPG|Sue ware jar
{{small|Tokyo National Museum}} File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 出土 須恵器 蓋付・須恵器 高台付椀.JPG|Sue ware lidded vessels and high-footed bowls

One of the largest {{ill|Yayoi|ja|弥生時代}} burial mounds in Japan. * Nihata Temple Site - Ruins of an ancient temple. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Explanatory panel (Kurashiki City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1974|chapter=Ōbosan Kofun (Akai Nishi No.1)|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/13111|title=Ōbosan Archaeological Sites|publisher=Kurashiki City Board of Education|pages=187–198}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Ōbosan Kofun|title=Okayama Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia]] Vol. 34|publisher=Heibonsha}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Obosan Kofun (Kurashiki)}} * [http://www.pref.okayama.jp/cgi-bin/bunka/culture/controller/client/resultDetail.cgi?id=336 Ōbosan Kofun] - Okayama Prefecture “Cultural Properties of Okayama” * [http://www.city.kurashiki.okayama.jp/5510.htm Ōbosan Kofun] - Kurashiki City Official Site [[Category:Kofun in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kurashiki]] [[Category:Circular tumuli]] [[Category:Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:long o change]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Obosan Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Obosan Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:long o change]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Obosan Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Obosan Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:long o change]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Obosan Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Obosan Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[ja:王墓山古墳]]

{{Ancient_Kofun_in_Japan |name=Ōbosan Kofun |image=[[File:Obosan Kofun (Kurashiki), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (sarcophagus visible at the far right) |Alternative_name=Akai Nishi No.1 Kofun |location={{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}{{ill|Kurashiki|ja|倉敷市}}, Nihata (Ōbo-no-Oka Historic Park) |coordinates={{Coord|34|39|35.10|N|133|49|35.34|E}} |type=Circular or square-shaped mound |Scale=Diameter/side length: 25m |Burial_Facility={{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室}} with a constructed house-shaped sarcophagus |Construction_Period=Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} |Artifacts=Numerous grave goods |Cultural_Property={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}, designated in 1959 |image2=[[File:Obosan Kofun (Kurashiki), sekkan.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped sarcophagus}}]] }} '''Ōbosan Kofun''' (王墓山古墳) is a burial mound located in {{ill|Kurashiki|ja|倉敷市}}, Nihata, Okayama Prefecture. It is part of the Ōbosan Kofun Cluster (or Akai Nishi Kofun Cluster) and is designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture. == Overview == Ōbosan Kofun is situated on a low hill along the right bank of the Ashimori River. This area is known for its concentration of burial mounds, with Ōbosan Kofun serving as the primary tumulus of the cluster. The mound and burial chamber underwent significant alterations during the late Meiji period. The original shape of the mound is estimated to have been either circular or square, with a diameter or side length of approximately 25 meters. The burial chamber was likely a horizontal stone chamber, although no trace of it remains today due to stone extraction. The site retains a house-shaped sarcophagus placed near the mound. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 6th century. The sarcophagus exhibits close similarities to that of the {{ill|Kōmorizuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, indicating a potential connection between the two sites. Ōbosan Kofun was designated a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture in 1959. Today, it is part of the Ōbo-no-Oka Historic Park and is open to the public. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is presumed to have been a horizontal stone chamber. However, the chamber was destroyed during the late Meiji period for stone quarrying, and only the sarcophagus remains. The sarcophagus, crafted from shell limestone sourced from {{ill|Ibara, Okayama|ja|井原市}}, is a constructed house-shaped type comprising seven stone slabs. Its dimensions are as follows: * Sarcophagus body: 2.05 meters in length * Sarcophagus material: Shell limestone (Namakata stone) == Artifacts == Grave goods unearthed from the site include: * A bronze mirror (decorated with Buddhist and animal motifs) * Gilded bronze horse trappings * Iron weapons * Ornaments * Pottery (Sue ware) These artifacts are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. However, it is suggested that some of the items may originate from neighboring burial mounds within the cluster. File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 出土 画文帯仏獣鏡.JPG|Bronze mirror
{{small|Exhibited at Tokyo National Museum}} File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 竪矧広板鋲留衝角付冑.JPG|Iron helmet with rivets
{{small|Tokyo National Museum}} File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 出土 鐘形杏葉.JPG|Bell-shaped horse gear
{{small|Tokyo National Museum}} File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 広口壺 (J-7646).JPG|Sue ware jar
{{small|Tokyo National Museum}} File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 出土 須恵器 蓋付・須恵器 高台付椀.JPG|Sue ware lidded vessels and high-footed bowls

revision.text:
{{Ancient_Kofun_in_Japan |name=Ōbosan Kofun |image=[[File:Obosan Kofun (Kurashiki), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (sarcophagus visible at the far right) |Alternative_name=Akai Nishi No.1 Kofun |location={{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}{{ill|Kurashiki|ja|倉敷市}}, Nihata (Ōbo-no-Oka Historic Park) |coordinates={{Coord|34|39|35.10|N|133|49|35.34|E}} |type=Circular or square-shaped mound |Scale=Diameter/side length: 25m |Burial_Facility={{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室}} with a constructed house-shaped sarcophagus |Construction_Period=Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} |Artifacts=Numerous grave goods |Cultural_Property={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}, designated in 1959 |image2=[[File:Obosan Kofun (Kurashiki), sekkan.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped sarcophagus}}]] }} '''Ōbosan Kofun''' (王墓山古墳) is a burial mound located in {{ill|Kurashiki|ja|倉敷市}}, Nihata, Okayama Prefecture. It is part of the Ōbosan Kofun Cluster (or Akai Nishi Kofun Cluster) and is designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture. == Overview == Ōbosan Kofun is situated on a low hill along the right bank of the Ashimori River. This area is known for its concentration of burial mounds, with Ōbosan Kofun serving as the primary tumulus of the cluster. The mound and burial chamber underwent significant alterations during the late Meiji period. The original shape of the mound is estimated to have been either circular or square, with a diameter or side length of approximately 25 meters. The burial chamber was likely a horizontal stone chamber, although no trace of it remains today due to stone extraction. The site retains a house-shaped sarcophagus placed near the mound. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 6th century. The sarcophagus exhibits close similarities to that of the {{ill|Kōmorizuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, indicating a potential connection between the two sites. Ōbosan Kofun was designated a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture in 1959. Today, it is part of the Ōbo-no-Oka Historic Park and is open to the public. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is presumed to have been a horizontal stone chamber. However, the chamber was destroyed during the late Meiji period for stone quarrying, and only the sarcophagus remains. The sarcophagus, crafted from shell limestone sourced from {{ill|Ibara, Okayama|ja|井原市}}, is a constructed house-shaped type comprising seven stone slabs. Its dimensions are as follows: * Sarcophagus body: 2.05 meters in length * Sarcophagus material: Shell limestone (Namakata stone) == Artifacts == Grave goods unearthed from the site include: * A bronze mirror (decorated with Buddhist and animal motifs) * Gilded bronze horse trappings * Iron weapons * Ornaments * Pottery (Sue ware) These artifacts are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. However, it is suggested that some of the items may originate from neighboring burial mounds within the cluster. File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 出土 画文帯仏獣鏡.JPG|Bronze mirror
{{small|Exhibited at Tokyo National Museum}} File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 竪矧広板鋲留衝角付冑.JPG|Iron helmet with rivets
{{small|Tokyo National Museum}} File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 出土 鐘形杏葉.JPG|Bell-shaped horse gear
{{small|Tokyo National Museum}} File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 広口壺 (J-7646).JPG|Sue ware jar
{{small|Tokyo National Museum}} File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 出土 須恵器 蓋付・須恵器 高台付椀.JPG|Sue ware lidded vessels and high-footed bowls

{{Ancient_Kofun_in_Japan |name=Ōbosan Kofun |image=[[File:Obosan Kofun (Kurashiki), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (sarcophagus visible at the far right) |Alternative_name=Akai Nishi No.1 Kofun |location={{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}{{ill|Kurashiki|ja|倉敷市}}, Nihata (Ōbo-no-Oka Historic Park) |coordinates={{Coord|34|39|35.10|N|133|49|35.34|E}} |type=Circular or square-shaped mound |Scale=Diameter/side length: 25m |Burial_Facility={{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室}} with a constructed house-shaped sarcophagus |Construction_Period=Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} |Artifacts=Numerous grave goods |Cultural_Property={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}, designated in 1959 |image2=[[File:Obosan Kofun (Kurashiki), sekkan.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped sarcophagus}}]] }} '''Ōbosan Kofun''' (王墓山古墳) is a burial mound located in {{ill|Kurashiki|ja|倉敷市}}, Nihata, Okayama Prefecture. It is part of the Ōbosan Kofun Cluster (or Akai Nishi Kofun Cluster) and is designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture. == Overview == Ōbosan Kofun is situated on a low hill along the right bank of the Ashimori River. This area is known for its concentration of burial mounds, with Ōbosan Kofun serving as the primary tumulus of the cluster. The mound and burial chamber underwent significant alterations during the late Meiji period. The original shape of the mound is estimated to have been either circular or square, with a diameter or side length of approximately 25 meters. The burial chamber was likely a horizontal stone chamber, although no trace of it remains today due to stone extraction. The site retains a house-shaped sarcophagus placed near the mound. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 6th century. The sarcophagus exhibits close similarities to that of the {{ill|Kōmorizuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, indicating a potential connection between the two sites. Ōbosan Kofun was designated a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture in 1959. Today, it is part of the Ōbo-no-Oka Historic Park and is open to the public. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is presumed to have been a horizontal stone chamber. However, the chamber was destroyed during the late Meiji period for stone quarrying, and only the sarcophagus remains. The sarcophagus, crafted from shell limestone sourced from {{ill|Ibara, Okayama|ja|井原市}}, is a constructed house-shaped type comprising seven stone slabs. Its dimensions are as follows: * Sarcophagus body: 2.05 meters in length * Sarcophagus material: Shell limestone (Namakata stone) == Artifacts == Grave goods unearthed from the site include: * A bronze mirror (decorated with Buddhist and animal motifs) * Gilded bronze horse trappings * Iron weapons * Ornaments * Pottery (Sue ware) These artifacts are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. However, it is suggested that some of the items may originate from neighboring burial mounds within the cluster. File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 出土 画文帯仏獣鏡.JPG|Bronze mirror

{{Ancient_Kofun_in_Japan |name=Ōbosan Kofun |image=[[File:Obosan Kofun (Kurashiki), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (sarcophagus visible at the far right) |Alternative_name=Akai Nishi No.1 Kofun |location={{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}{{ill|Kurashiki|ja|倉敷市}}, Nihata (Ōbo-no-Oka Historic Park) |coordinates={{Coord|34|39|35.10|N|133|49|35.34|E}} |type=Circular or square-shaped mound |Scale=Diameter/side length: 25m |Burial_Facility={{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室}} with a constructed house-shaped sarcophagus |Construction_Period=Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} |Artifacts=Numerous grave goods |Cultural_Property={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}, designated in 1959 |image2=[[File:Obosan Kofun (Kurashiki), sekkan.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped sarcophagus}}]] }} '''Ōbosan Kofun''' (王墓山古墳) is a burial mound located in {{ill|Kurashiki|ja|倉敷市}}, Nihata, Okayama Prefecture. It is part of the Ōbosan Kofun Cluster (or Akai Nishi Kofun Cluster) and is designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture. == Overview == Ōbosan Kofun is situated on a low hill along the right bank of the Ashimori River. This area is known for its concentration of burial mounds, with Ōbosan Kofun serving as the primary tumulus of the cluster. The mound and burial chamber underwent significant alterations during the late Meiji period. The original shape of the mound is estimated to have been either circular or square, with a diameter or side length of approximately 25 meters. The burial chamber was likely a horizontal stone chamber, although no trace of it remains today due to stone extraction. The site retains a house-shaped sarcophagus placed near the mound. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 6th century. The sarcophagus exhibits close similarities to that of the {{ill|Kōmorizuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, indicating a potential connection between the two sites. Ōbosan Kofun was designated a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture in 1959. Today, it is part of the Ōbo-no-Oka Historic Park and is open to the public. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is presumed to have been a horizontal stone chamber. However, the chamber was destroyed during the late Meiji period for stone quarrying, and only the sarcophagus remains. The sarcophagus, crafted from shell limestone sourced from {{ill|Ibara, Okayama|ja|井原市}}, is a constructed house-shaped type comprising seven stone slabs. Its dimensions are as follows: * Sarcophagus body: 2.05 meters in length * Sarcophagus material: Shell limestone (Namakata stone) == Artifacts == Grave goods unearthed from the site include: * A bronze mirror (decorated with Buddhist and animal motifs) * Gilded bronze horse trappings * Iron weapons * Ornaments * Pottery (Sue ware) These artifacts are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. However, it is suggested that some of the items may originate from neighboring burial mounds within the cluster. File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 出土 画文帯仏獣鏡.JPG|Bronze mirror

{{small|Tokyo National Museum}} File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 出土 鐘形杏葉.JPG|Bell-shaped horse gear
{{small|Tokyo National Museum}} File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 広口壺 (J-7646).JPG|Sue ware jar
{{small|Tokyo National Museum}} File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 出土 須恵器 蓋付・須恵器 高台付椀.JPG|Sue ware lidded vessels and high-footed bowls

This area is known for its concentration of burial mounds, with Ōbosan Kofun serving as the primary tumulus of the cluster. The mound and burial chamber underwent significant alterations during the late Meiji period. The original shape of the mound is estimated to have been either circular or square, with a diameter or side length of approximately 25 meters. The burial chamber was likely a horizontal stone chamber, although no trace of it remains today due to stone extraction. The site retains a house-shaped sarcophagus placed near the mound. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 6th century. The sarcophagus exhibits close similarities to that of the {{ill|Kōmorizuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, indicating a potential connection between the two sites. Ōbosan Kofun was designated a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture in 1959. Today, it is part of the Ōbo-no-Oka Historic Park and is open to the public. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is presumed to have been a horizontal stone chamber. Mound (sarcophagus visible at the far right) |Alternative_name=Akai Nishi No.1 Kofun |location={{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}{{ill|Kurashiki|ja|倉敷市}}, Nihata (Ōbo-no-Oka Historic Park) |coordinates={{Coord|34|39|35.10|N|133|49|35.34|E}} |type=Circular or square-shaped mound |Scale=Diameter/side length: 25m |Burial_Facility={{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室}} with a constructed house-shaped sarcophagus |Construction_Period=Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} |Artifacts=Numerous grave goods |Cultural_Property={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}, designated in 1959 |image2=[[File:Obosan Kofun (Kurashiki), sekkan.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped sarcophagus}}]] }} '''Ōbosan Kofun''' (王墓山古墳) is a burial mound located in {{ill|Kurashiki|ja|倉敷市}}, Nihata, Okayama Prefecture. It is part of the Ōbosan Kofun Cluster (or Akai Nishi Kofun Cluster) and is designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture. == Overview == Ōbosan Kofun is situated on a low hill along the right bank of the Ashimori River. This area is known for its concentration of burial mounds, with Ōbosan Kofun serving as the primary tumulus of the cluster. The mound and burial chamber underwent significant alterations during the late Meiji period. The original shape of the mound is estimated to have been either circular or square, with a diameter or side length of approximately 25 meters. The burial chamber was likely a horizontal stone chamber, although no trace of it remains today due to stone extraction. The site retains a house-shaped sarcophagus placed near the mound. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 6th century. The sarcophagus exhibits close similarities to that of the {{ill|Kōmorizuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, indicating a potential connection between the two sites. Ōbosan Kofun was designated a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture in 1959. Today, it is part of the Ōbo-no-Oka Historic Park and is open to the public. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is presumed to have been a horizontal stone chamber. However, the chamber was destroyed during the late Meiji period for stone quarrying, and only the sarcophagus remains. The sarcophagus, crafted from shell limestone sourced from {{ill|Ibara, Okayama|ja|井原市}}, is a constructed house-shaped type comprising seven stone slabs. Its dimensions are as follows: * Sarcophagus body: 2.05 meters in length * Sarcophagus material: Shell limestone (Namakata stone) == Artifacts == Grave goods unearthed from the site include: * A bronze mirror (decorated with Buddhist and animal motifs) * Gilded bronze horse trappings * Iron weapons * Ornaments * Pottery (Sue ware) These artifacts are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. However, it is suggested that some of the items may originate from neighboring burial mounds within the cluster. File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 出土 画文帯仏獣鏡.JPG|Bronze mirror
{{small|Exhibited at Tokyo National Museum}} File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 竪矧広板鋲留衝角付冑.JPG|Iron helmet with rivets
{{small|Tokyo National Museum}} File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 出土 鐘形杏葉.JPG|Bell-shaped horse gear
{{small|Tokyo National Museum}} File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 広口壺 (J-7646).JPG|Sue ware jar
{{small|Tokyo National Museum}} File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 出土 須恵器 蓋付・須恵器 高台付椀.JPG|Sue ware lidded vessels and high-footed bowls
{{small|Tokyo National Museum}} == Cultural Properties == === Okayama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Ōbosan Kofun - Designated on March 27, 1959[http://www.pref.okayama.jp/cgi-bin/bunka/culture/controller/client/resultDetail.cgi?id=336 Ōbosan Kofun] (Okayama Prefecture “Cultural Properties of Okayama”).. == Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Tatetsuki Site|ja|楯築墳丘墓}} - One of the largest {{ill|Yayoi|ja|弥生時代}} burial mounds in Japan. * Nihata Temple Site - Ruins of an ancient temple. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Explanatory panel (Kurashiki City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1974|chapter=Ōbosan Kofun (Akai Nishi No.1)|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/13111|title=Ōbosan Archaeological Sites|publisher=Kurashiki City Board of Education|pages=187–198}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Ōbosan Kofun|title=Okayama Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia]] Vol. 34|publisher=Heibonsha}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Obosan Kofun (Kurashiki)}} * [http://www.pref.okayama.jp/cgi-bin/bunka/culture/controller/client/resultDetail.cgi?id=336 Ōbosan Kofun] - {{Ancient_Kofun_in_Japan |name=Ōbosan Kofun |image=[[File:Obosan Kofun (Kurashiki), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (sarcophagus visible at the far right) |Alternative_name=Akai Nishi No.1 Kofun |location={{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}{{ill|Kurashiki|ja|倉敷市}}, Nihata (Ōbo-no-Oka Historic Park) |coordinates={{Coord|34|39|35.10|N|133|49|35.34|E}} |type=Circular or square-shaped mound |Scale=Diameter/side length: 25m |Burial_Facility={{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室}} with a constructed house-shaped sarcophagus |Construction_Period=Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} |Artifacts=Numerous grave goods |Cultural_Property={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}, designated in 1959 |image2=[[File:Obosan Kofun (Kurashiki), sekkan.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped sarcophagus}}]] }} '''Ōbosan Kofun''' (王墓山古墳) is a burial mound located in {{ill|Kurashiki|ja|倉敷市}}, Nihata, Okayama Prefecture. It is part of the Ōbosan Kofun Cluster (or Akai Nishi Kofun Cluster) and is designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture. == Overview == Ōbosan Kofun is situated on a low hill along the right bank of the Ashimori River. This area is known for its concentration of burial mounds, with Ōbosan Kofun serving as the primary tumulus of the cluster. The mound and burial chamber underwent significant alterations during the late Meiji period. The original shape of the mound is estimated to have been either circular or square, with a diameter or side length of approximately 25 meters. The burial chamber was likely a horizontal stone chamber, although no trace of it remains today due to stone extraction. The site retains a house-shaped sarcophagus placed near the mound. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 6th century. The sarcophagus exhibits close similarities to that of the {{ill|Kōmorizuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, indicating a potential connection between the two sites. Ōbosan Kofun was designated a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture in 1959. Today, it is part of the Ōbo-no-Oka Historic Park and is open to the public. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is presumed to have been a horizontal stone chamber. However, the chamber was destroyed during the late Meiji period for stone quarrying, and only the sarcophagus remains. The sarcophagus, crafted from shell limestone sourced from {{ill|Ibara, Okayama|ja|井原市}}, is a constructed house-shaped type comprising seven stone slabs. Its dimensions are as follows: * Sarcophagus body: 2.05 meters in length * Sarcophagus material: Shell limestone (Namakata stone) == Artifacts == Grave goods unearthed from the site include: * A bronze mirror (decorated with Buddhist and animal motifs) * Gilded bronze horse trappings * Iron weapons * Ornaments * Pottery (Sue ware) These artifacts are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. However, it is suggested that some of the items may originate from neighboring burial mounds within the cluster. File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 出土 画文帯仏獣鏡.JPG|Bronze mirror
{{small|Exhibited at Tokyo National Museum}} File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 竪矧広板鋲留衝角付冑.JPG|Iron helmet with rivets
{{small|Tokyo National Museum}} File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 出土 鐘形杏葉.JPG|Bell-shaped horse gear
{{small|Tokyo National Museum}} File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 広口壺 (J-7646).JPG|Sue ware jar
{{small|Tokyo National Museum}} File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 出土 須恵器 蓋付・須恵器 高台付椀.JPG|Sue ware lidded vessels and high-footed bowls
{{small|Tokyo National Museum}} == Cultural Properties == === Okayama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Ōbosan Kofun - Designated on March 27, 1959[http://www.pref.okayama.jp/cgi-bin/bunka/culture/controller/client/resultDetail.cgi?id=336 Ōbosan Kofun] (Okayama Prefecture “Cultural Properties of Okayama”).. == Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Tatetsuki Site|ja|楯築墳丘墓}} - One of the largest {{ill|Yayoi|ja|弥生時代}} burial mounds in Japan. * Nihata Temple Site - Ruins of an ancient temple. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Explanatory panel (Kurashiki City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1974|chapter=Ōbosan Kofun (Akai Nishi No.1)|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/13111|title=Ōbosan Archaeological Sites|publisher=Kurashiki City Board of Education|pages=187–198}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Ōbosan Kofun|title=Okayama Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia]] Vol. 34|publisher=Heibonsha}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Obosan Kofun (Kurashiki)}} * [http://www.pref.okayama.jp/cgi-bin/bunka/culture/controller/client/resultDetail.cgi?id=336 Ōbosan Kofun] - It is part of the Ōbosan Kofun Cluster (or Akai Nishi Kofun Cluster) and is designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture. == Overview == Ōbosan Kofun is situated on a low hill along the right bank of the Ashimori River. This area is known for its concentration of burial mounds, with Ōbosan Kofun serving as the primary tumulus of the cluster. The mound and burial chamber underwent significant alterations during the late Meiji period. The original shape of the mound is estimated to have been either circular or square, with a diameter or side length of approximately 25 meters. The burial chamber was likely a horizontal stone chamber, although no trace of it remains today due to stone extraction. The site retains a house-shaped sarcophagus placed near the mound. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 6th century. The sarcophagus exhibits close similarities to that of the {{ill|Kōmorizuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, indicating a potential connection between the two sites. Ōbosan Kofun was designated a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture in 1959. Today, it is part of the Ōbo-no-Oka Historic Park and is open to the public. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is presumed to have been a horizontal stone chamber. {{Ancient_Kofun_in_Japan |name=Ōbosan Kofun |image=[[File:Obosan Kofun (Kurashiki), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (sarcophagus visible at the far right) |Alternative_name=Akai Nishi No.1 Kofun |location={{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}{{ill|Kurashiki|ja|倉敷市}}, Nihata (Ōbo-no-Oka Historic Park) |coordinates={{Coord|34|39|35.10|N|133|49|35.34|E}} |type=Circular or square-shaped mound |Scale=Diameter/side length: 25m |Burial_Facility={{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室}} with a constructed house-shaped sarcophagus |Construction_Period=Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} |Artifacts=Numerous grave goods |Cultural_Property={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}, designated in 1959 |image2=[[File:Obosan Kofun (Kurashiki), sekkan.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped sarcophagus}}]] }} '''Ōbosan Kofun''' (王墓山古墳) is a burial mound located in {{ill|Kurashiki|ja|倉敷市}}, Nihata, Okayama Prefecture. It is part of the Ōbosan Kofun Cluster (or Akai Nishi Kofun Cluster) and is designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture. == Overview == Ōbosan Kofun is situated on a low hill along the right bank of the Ashimori River. This area is known for its concentration of burial mounds, with Ōbosan Kofun serving as the primary tumulus of the cluster. The mound and burial chamber underwent significant alterations during the late Meiji period. The original shape of the mound is estimated to have been either circular or square, with a diameter or side length of approximately 25 meters. The burial chamber was likely a horizontal stone chamber, although no trace of it remains today due to stone extraction. The site retains a house-shaped sarcophagus placed near the mound. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 6th century. The sarcophagus exhibits close similarities to that of the {{ill|Kōmorizuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, indicating a potential connection between the two sites. Ōbosan Kofun was designated a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture in 1959. Today, it is part of the Ōbo-no-Oka Historic Park and is open to the public. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is presumed to have been a horizontal stone chamber. However, the chamber was destroyed during the late Meiji period for stone quarrying, and only the sarcophagus remains. The sarcophagus, crafted from shell limestone sourced from {{ill|Ibara, Okayama|ja|井原市}}, is a constructed house-shaped type comprising seven stone slabs. Its dimensions are as follows: * Sarcophagus body: 2.05 meters in length * Sarcophagus material: Shell limestone (Namakata stone) == Artifacts == Grave goods unearthed from the site include: * A bronze mirror (decorated with Buddhist and animal motifs) * Gilded bronze horse trappings * Iron weapons * Ornaments * Pottery (Sue ware) These artifacts are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. However, it is suggested that some of the items may originate from neighboring burial mounds within the cluster. File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 出土 画文帯仏獣鏡.JPG|Bronze mirror

{{small|Tokyo National Museum}} File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 出土 鐘形杏葉.JPG|Bell-shaped horse gear
{{small|Tokyo National Museum}} File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 広口壺 (J-7646).JPG|Sue ware jar
{{small|Tokyo National Museum}} File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 出土 須恵器 蓋付・須恵器 高台付椀.JPG|Sue ware lidded vessels and high-footed bowls

It is part of the Ōbosan Kofun Cluster (or Akai Nishi Kofun Cluster) and is designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture. == Overview == Ōbosan Kofun is situated on a low hill along the right bank of the Ashimori River. This area is known for its concentration of burial mounds, with Ōbosan Kofun serving as the primary tumulus of the cluster. The mound and burial chamber underwent significant alterations during the late Meiji period. The original shape of the mound is estimated to have been either circular or square, with a diameter or side length of approximately 25 meters. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 6th century. The sarcophagus exhibits close similarities to that of the {{ill|Kōmorizuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, indicating a potential connection between the two sites. It is part of the Ōbosan Kofun Cluster (or Akai Nishi Kofun Cluster) and is designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture. == Overview == Ōbosan Kofun is situated on a low hill along the right bank of the Ashimori River. This area is known for its concentration of burial mounds, with Ōbosan Kofun serving as the primary tumulus of the cluster. The mound and burial chamber underwent significant alterations during the late Meiji period. The original shape of the mound is estimated to have been either circular or square, with a diameter or side length of approximately 25 meters. The burial chamber was likely a horizontal stone chamber, although no trace of it remains today due to stone extraction. The site retains a house-shaped sarcophagus placed near the mound. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 6th century. The sarcophagus exhibits close similarities to that of the {{ill|Kōmorizuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, indicating a potential connection between the two sites. Ōbosan Kofun was designated a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture in 1959. Today, it is part of the Ōbo-no-Oka Historic Park and is open to the public. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is presumed to have been a horizontal stone chamber. revision.text:
{{Ancient_Kofun_in_Japan |name=Ōbosan Kofun |image=[[File:Obosan Kofun (Kurashiki), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (sarcophagus visible at the far right) |Alternative_name=Akai Nishi No.1 Kofun |location={{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}{{ill|Kurashiki|ja|倉敷市}}, Nihata (Ōbo-no-Oka Historic Park) |coordinates={{Coord|34|39|35.10|N|133|49|35.34|E}} |type=Circular or square-shaped mound |Scale=Diameter/side length: 25m |Burial_Facility={{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室}} with a constructed house-shaped sarcophagus |Construction_Period=Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} |Artifacts=Numerous grave goods |Cultural_Property={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}, designated in 1959 |image2=[[File:Obosan Kofun (Kurashiki), sekkan.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped sarcophagus}}]] }} '''Ōbosan Kofun''' (王墓山古墳) is a burial mound located in {{ill|Kurashiki|ja|倉敷市}}, Nihata, Okayama Prefecture. It is part of the Ōbosan Kofun Cluster (or Akai Nishi Kofun Cluster) and is designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture. == Overview == Ōbosan Kofun is situated on a low hill along the right bank of the Ashimori River. This area is known for its concentration of burial mounds, with Ōbosan Kofun serving as the primary tumulus of the cluster. The mound and burial chamber underwent significant alterations during the late Meiji period. The original shape of the mound is estimated to have been either circular or square, with a diameter or side length of approximately 25 meters. The burial chamber was likely a horizontal stone chamber, although no trace of it remains today due to stone extraction. The site retains a house-shaped sarcophagus placed near the mound. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 6th century. The sarcophagus exhibits close similarities to that of the {{ill|Kōmorizuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, indicating a potential connection between the two sites. Ōbosan Kofun was designated a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture in 1959. Today, it is part of the Ōbo-no-Oka Historic Park and is open to the public. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is presumed to have been a horizontal stone chamber. However, the chamber was destroyed during the late Meiji period for stone quarrying, and only the sarcophagus remains. The sarcophagus, crafted from shell limestone sourced from {{ill|Ibara, Okayama|ja|井原市}}, is a constructed house-shaped type comprising seven stone slabs. Its dimensions are as follows: * Sarcophagus body: 2.05 meters in length * Sarcophagus material: Shell limestone (Namakata stone) == Artifacts == Grave goods unearthed from the site include: * A bronze mirror (decorated with Buddhist and animal motifs) * Gilded bronze horse trappings * Iron weapons * Ornaments * Pottery (Sue ware) These artifacts are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. However, it is suggested that some of the items may originate from neighboring burial mounds within the cluster. File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 出土 画文帯仏獣鏡.JPG|Bronze mirror
{{small|Exhibited at Tokyo National Museum}} File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 竪矧広板鋲留衝角付冑.JPG|Iron helmet with rivets
{{small|Tokyo National Museum}} File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 出土 鐘形杏葉.JPG|Bell-shaped horse gear
{{small|Tokyo National Museum}} File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 広口壺 (J-7646).JPG|Sue ware jar
{{small|Tokyo National Museum}} File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 出土 須恵器 蓋付・須恵器 高台付椀.JPG|Sue ware lidded vessels and high-footed bowls

{{ill|Tatetsuki Site|ja|楯築墳丘墓}} - One of the largest {{ill|Yayoi|ja|弥生時代}} burial mounds in Japan. * Nihata Temple Site - Ruins of an ancient temple. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Mound (sarcophagus visible at the far right) |Alternative_name=Akai Nishi No.1 Kofun |location={{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}{{ill|Kurashiki|ja|倉敷市}}, Nihata (Ōbo-no-Oka Historic Park) |coordinates={{Coord|34|39|35.10|N|133|49|35.34|E}} |type=Circular or square-shaped mound |Scale=Diameter/side length: 25m |Burial_Facility={{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室}} with a constructed house-shaped sarcophagus |Construction_Period=Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} |Artifacts=Numerous grave goods |Cultural_Property={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}, designated in 1959 |image2=[[File:Obosan Kofun (Kurashiki), sekkan.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped sarcophagus}}]] }} '''Ōbosan Kofun''' (王墓山古墳) is a burial mound located in {{ill|Kurashiki|ja|倉敷市}}, Nihata, Okayama Prefecture. {{Ancient_Kofun_in_Japan |name=Ōbosan Kofun |image=[[File:Obosan Kofun (Kurashiki), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (sarcophagus visible at the far right) |Alternative_name=Akai Nishi No.1 Kofun |location={{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}{{ill|Kurashiki|ja|倉敷市}}, Nihata (Ōbo-no-Oka Historic Park) |coordinates={{Coord|34|39|35.10|N|133|49|35.34|E}} |type=Circular or square-shaped mound |Scale=Diameter/side length: 25m |Burial_Facility={{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室}} with a constructed house-shaped sarcophagus |Construction_Period=Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} |Artifacts=Numerous grave goods |Cultural_Property={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}, designated in 1959 |image2=[[File:Obosan Kofun (Kurashiki), sekkan.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped sarcophagus}}]] }} '''Ōbosan Kofun''' (王墓山古墳) is a burial mound located in {{ill|Kurashiki|ja|倉敷市}}, Nihata, Okayama Prefecture. It is part of the Ōbosan Kofun Cluster (or Akai Nishi Kofun Cluster) and is designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture. == Overview == Ōbosan Kofun is situated on a low hill along the right bank of the Ashimori River. This area is known for its concentration of burial mounds, with Ōbosan Kofun serving as the primary tumulus of the cluster. The mound and burial chamber underwent significant alterations during the late Meiji period. The original shape of the mound is estimated to have been either circular or square, with a diameter or side length of approximately 25 meters. The burial chamber was likely a horizontal stone chamber, although no trace of it remains today due to stone extraction. The site retains a house-shaped sarcophagus placed near the mound. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 6th century. The sarcophagus exhibits close similarities to that of the {{ill|Kōmorizuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, indicating a potential connection between the two sites. Ōbosan Kofun was designated a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture in 1959. Today, it is part of the Ōbo-no-Oka Historic Park and is open to the public. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is presumed to have been a horizontal stone chamber. However, the chamber was destroyed during the late Meiji period for stone quarrying, and only the sarcophagus remains. The sarcophagus, crafted from shell limestone sourced from {{ill|Ibara, Okayama|ja|井原市}}, is a constructed house-shaped type comprising seven stone slabs. Its dimensions are as follows: * Sarcophagus body: 2.05 meters in length * Sarcophagus material: Shell limestone (Namakata stone) == Artifacts == Grave goods unearthed from the site include: * A bronze mirror (decorated with Buddhist and animal motifs) * Gilded bronze horse trappings * Iron weapons * Ornaments * Pottery (Sue ware) These artifacts are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. However, it is suggested that some of the items may originate from neighboring burial mounds within the cluster. File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 出土 画文帯仏獣鏡.JPG|Bronze mirror
{{small|Exhibited at Tokyo National Museum}} File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 竪矧広板鋲留衝角付冑.JPG|Iron helmet with rivets

{{small|Tokyo National Museum}} File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 広口壺 (J-7646).JPG|Sue ware jar
{{small|Tokyo National Museum}} File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 出土 須恵器 蓋付・須恵器 高台付椀.JPG|Sue ware lidded vessels and high-footed bowls

1959[http://www.pref.okayama.jp/cgi-bin/bunka/culture/controller/client/resultDetail.cgi?id=336 Ōbosan Kofun] (Okayama Prefecture “Cultural Properties of Okayama”).. == Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Tatetsuki Site|ja|楯築墳丘墓}} - One of the largest {{ill|Yayoi|ja|弥生時代}} burial mounds in Japan. * Nihata Temple Site - Ruins of an ancient temple. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Ancient_Kofun_in_Japan |name=Ōbosan Kofun |image=[[File:Obosan Kofun (Kurashiki), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (sarcophagus visible at the far right) |Alternative_name=Akai Nishi No.1 Kofun |location={{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}{{ill|Kurashiki|ja|倉敷市}}, Nihata (Ōbo-no-Oka Historic Park) |coordinates={{Coord|34|39|35.10|N|133|49|35.34|E}} |type=Circular or square-shaped mound |Scale=Diameter/side length: 25m |Burial_Facility={{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} with a constructed house-shaped sarcophagus |Construction_Period=Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} |Artifacts=Numerous grave goods |Cultural_Property={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}, designated in 1959 |image2=[[File:Obosan Kofun (Kurashiki), sekkan.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped sarcophagus}}]] }} '''Ōbosan Kofun''' (王墓山古墳) is a burial mound located in {{ill|Kurashiki|ja|倉敷市}}, Nihata, Okayama Prefecture. It is part of the Ōbosan Kofun Cluster (or Akai Nishi Kofun Cluster) and is designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture. == Overview == Ōbosan Kofun is situated on a low hill along the right bank of the Ashimori River. This area is known for its concentration of burial mounds, with Ōbosan Kofun serving as the primary tumulus of the cluster. The mound and burial chamber underwent significant alterations during the late Meiji period. The original shape of the mound is estimated to have been either circular or square, with a diameter or side length of approximately 25 meters. The burial chamber was likely a horizontal stone chamber, although no trace of it remains today due to stone extraction. The site retains a house-shaped sarcophagus placed near the mound. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 6th century. The sarcophagus exhibits close similarities to that of the {{ill|Kōmorizuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, indicating a potential connection between the two sites. Ōbosan Kofun was designated a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture in 1959. Today, it is part of the Ōbo-no-Oka Historic Park and is open to the public. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is presumed to have been a horizontal stone chamber. However, the chamber was destroyed during the late Meiji period for stone quarrying, and only the sarcophagus remains. The sarcophagus, crafted from shell limestone sourced from {{ill|Ibara, Okayama|ja|井原市}}, is a constructed house-shaped type comprising seven stone slabs. Its dimensions are as follows: * Sarcophagus body: 2.05 meters in length * Sarcophagus material: Shell limestone (Namakata stone) == Artifacts == Grave goods unearthed from the site include: * A bronze mirror (decorated with Buddhist and animal motifs) * Gilded bronze horse trappings * Iron weapons * Ornaments * Pottery (Sue ware) These artifacts are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. However, it is suggested that some of the items may originate from neighboring burial mounds within the cluster. File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 出土 画文帯仏獣鏡.JPG|Bronze mirror
{{small|Exhibited at Tokyo National Museum}} File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 竪矧広板鋲留衝角付冑.JPG|Iron helmet with rivets
{{small|Tokyo National Museum}} File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 出土 鐘形杏葉.JPG|Bell-shaped horse gear
{{small|Tokyo National Museum}} File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 広口壺 (J-7646).JPG|Sue ware jar
{{small|Tokyo National Museum}} File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 出土 須恵器 蓋付・須恵器 高台付椀.JPG|Sue ware lidded vessels and high-footed bowls
{{small|Tokyo National Museum}} == Cultural Properties == === Okayama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Ōbosan Kofun - Designated on March 27, 1959[http://www.pref.okayama.jp/cgi-bin/bunka/culture/controller/client/resultDetail.cgi?id=336 Ōbosan Kofun] (Okayama Prefecture “Cultural Properties of Okayama”).. == Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Tatetsuki Site|ja|楯築墳丘墓}} - One of the largest {{ill|Yayoi|ja|弥生時代}} burial mounds in Japan. * Nihata Temple Site - Ruins of an ancient temple. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Archaeological Sites|publisher=Kurashiki City Board of Education|pages=187–198}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Ōbosan Kofun|title=Okayama Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japanese The sarcophagus exhibits close similarities to that of the {{ill|Kōmorizuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, indicating a potential connection between the two sites. Ōbosan Kofun was designated a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture in 1959. Mound (sarcophagus visible at the far right) |Alternative_name=Akai Nishi No.1 Kofun |location={{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}{{ill|Kurashiki|ja|倉敷市}}, Nihata (Ōbo-no-Oka Historic Park) |coordinates={{Coord|34|39|35.10|N|133|49|35.34|E}} |type=Circular or square-shaped mound |Scale=Diameter/side length: 25m |Burial_Facility={{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} with a constructed house-shaped sarcophagus |Construction_Period=Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} |Artifacts=Numerous grave goods |Cultural_Property={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}, designated in 1959 |image2=[[File:Obosan Kofun (Kurashiki), sekkan.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped sarcophagus}}]] }} '''Ōbosan Kofun''' (王墓山古墳) is a burial mound located in {{ill|Kurashiki|ja|倉敷市}}, Nihata, Okayama Prefecture. It is part of the Ōbosan Kofun Cluster (or Akai Nishi Kofun Cluster) and is designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture. == Overview == Ōbosan Kofun is situated on a low hill along the right bank of the Ashimori River. This area is known for its concentration of burial mounds, with Ōbosan Kofun serving as the primary tumulus of the cluster. The mound and burial chamber underwent significant alterations during the late Meiji period. The original shape of the mound is estimated to have been either circular or square, with a diameter or side length of approximately 25 meters. The burial chamber was likely a horizontal stone chamber, although no trace of it remains today due to stone extraction. The site retains a house-shaped sarcophagus placed near the mound. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 6th century. The sarcophagus exhibits close similarities to that of the {{ill|Kōmorizuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, indicating a potential connection between the two sites. Ōbosan Kofun was designated a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture in 1959. Today, it is part of the Ōbo-no-Oka Historic Park and is open to the public. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is presumed to have been a horizontal stone chamber. However, the chamber was destroyed during the late Meiji period for stone quarrying, and only the sarcophagus remains. The sarcophagus, crafted from shell limestone sourced from {{ill|Ibara, Okayama|ja|井原市}}, is a constructed house-shaped type comprising seven stone slabs. Its dimensions are as follows: * Sarcophagus body: 2.05 meters in length * Sarcophagus material: Shell limestone (Namakata stone) == Artifacts == Grave goods unearthed from the site include: * A bronze mirror (decorated with Buddhist and animal motifs) * Gilded bronze horse trappings * Iron weapons * Ornaments * Pottery (Sue ware) These artifacts are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. However, it is suggested that some of the items may originate from neighboring burial mounds within the cluster. File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 出土 画文帯仏獣鏡.JPG|Bronze mirror

The mound and burial chamber underwent significant alterations during the late Meiji period. The original shape of the mound is estimated to have been either circular or square, with a diameter or side length of approximately 25 meters. The burial chamber was likely a horizontal stone chamber, although no trace of it remains today due to stone extraction. The site retains a house-shaped sarcophagus placed near the mound. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 6th century. The sarcophagus exhibits close similarities to that of the {{ill|Kōmorizuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, indicating a potential connection between the two sites. Ōbosan Kofun was designated a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture in 1959. Today, it is part of the Ōbo-no-Oka Historic Park and is open to the public. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is presumed to have been a horizontal stone chamber. However, the chamber was destroyed during the late Meiji period for stone quarrying, and only the sarcophagus remains. The sarcophagus, crafted from shell limestone sourced from {{ill|Ibara, Okayama|ja|井原市}}, is a constructed house-shaped type comprising seven stone slabs. Its dimensions are as follows: * Sarcophagus body: 2.05 meters in length * Sarcophagus material: Shell limestone (Namakata stone) == Artifacts == Grave goods unearthed from the site include: * A bronze mirror (decorated with Buddhist and animal motifs) * Gilded bronze horse trappings * Iron weapons * Ornaments * Pottery (Sue ware) These artifacts are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. However, it is suggested that some of the items may originate from neighboring burial mounds within the cluster. File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 出土 画文帯仏獣鏡.JPG|Bronze mirror

{{Ancient_Kofun_in_Japan |name=Ōbosan Kofun |image=[[File:Obosan Kofun (Kurashiki), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (sarcophagus visible at the far right) |Alternative_name=Akai Nishi No.1 Kofun |location={{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}{{ill|Kurashiki|ja|倉敷市}}, Nihata (Ōbo-no-Oka Historic Park) |coordinates={{Coord|34|39|35.10|N|133|49|35.34|E}} |type=Circular or square-shaped mound |Scale=Diameter/side length: 25m |Burial_Facility={{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} with a constructed house-shaped sarcophagus |Construction_Period=Late {{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}} |Artifacts=Numerous grave goods |Cultural_Property={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡}}, designated in 1959 |image2=[[File:Obosan Kofun (Kurashiki), sekkan.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped sarcophagus}}]] }} '''Ōbosan Kofun''' (王墓山古墳) is a burial mound located in {{ill|Kurashiki|ja|倉敷市}}, Nihata, Okayama Prefecture. It is part of the Ōbosan Kofun Cluster (or Akai Nishi Kofun Cluster) and is designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture. == Overview == Ōbosan Kofun is situated on a low hill along the right bank of the Ashimori River. This area is known for its concentration of burial mounds, with Ōbosan Kofun serving as the primary tumulus of the cluster. The mound and burial chamber underwent significant alterations during the late Meiji period. The original shape of the mound is estimated to have been either circular or square, with a diameter or side length of approximately 25 meters. The burial chamber was likely a horizontal stone chamber, although no trace of it remains today due to stone extraction. The site retains a house-shaped sarcophagus placed near the mound. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 6th century. The sarcophagus exhibits close similarities to that of the {{ill|Kōmorizuka Kofun|ja|こうもり塚古墳}} in {{ill|Sōja|ja|総社市}}, indicating a potential connection between the two sites. Ōbosan Kofun was designated a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture in 1959. Today, it is part of the Ōbo-no-Oka Historic Park and is open to the public. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is presumed to have been a horizontal stone chamber. However, the chamber was destroyed during the late Meiji period for stone quarrying, and only the sarcophagus remains. The sarcophagus, crafted from shell limestone sourced from {{ill|Ibara, Okayama|ja|井原市}}, is a constructed house-shaped type comprising seven stone slabs. Its dimensions are as follows: * Sarcophagus body: 2.05 meters in length * Sarcophagus material: Shell limestone (Namakata stone) == Artifacts == Grave goods unearthed from the site include: * A bronze mirror (decorated with Buddhist and animal motifs) * Gilded bronze horse trappings * Iron weapons * Ornaments * Pottery (Sue ware) These artifacts are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. However, it is suggested that some of the items may originate from neighboring burial mounds within the cluster. File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 出土 画文帯仏獣鏡.JPG|Bronze mirror
