Mound (sarcophagus visible at the far right) |alternate_name=Akai Nishi No.1 Kofun |location={{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}}{{ill|Kurashiki|en|Kurashiki|WD=Q467487|lt=Kurashiki|1=Kurashiki}}, Nihata (Ōbo-no-Oka Historic Park) |coordinates={{Coord|34|39|35.10|N|133|49|35.34|E}} |type=Circular or square-shaped mound |map_size=Diameter/side length: 25m |Burial Facility={{ill|Yokoana|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with a constructed house-shaped sarcophagus |built=Late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|WD=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}} |burial goods=Numerous grave goods |Historic Site={{ill|Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|ja|岡山県指定文化財一覧#史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|1=Okayama Prefecture Designated Historic Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11472269}}, designated in 1959 |image=[[File:Obosan Kofun (Kurashiki), sekkan.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|House-shaped sarcophagus}}]] }} '''Ōbosan Kofun''' (王墓山古墳) is a Kofun located in {{ill|Kurashiki|en|Kurashiki|WD=Q467487|lt=Kurashiki|1=Kurashiki}}, Nihata, Okayama Prefecture. It is part of the Ōbosan Kofun Cluster (or Akai Nishi Kofun Cluster) and is designated as a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture. == Overview == Ōbosan Kofun is situated on a low hill along the right bank of the Ashimori River. This area is known for its concentration of Kofuns, with Ōbosan Kofun serving as the primary tumulus of the cluster. The mound and burial chamber underwent significant alterations during the late Meiji period. The original shape of the mound is estimated to have been either circular or square, with a diameter or side length of approximately 25 meters. The burial chamber was likely a horizontal stone chamber, although no trace of it remains today due to stone extraction. The site retains a house-shaped sarcophagus placed near the mound. The tumulus is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}, around the late 6th century. The sarcophagus exhibits close similarities to that of the {{ill|Kōmorizuka Kofun|en|Kōmorizuka Kofun|WD=Q11266204|lt=Kōmorizuka Kofun|1=Kōmorizuka Kofun}} in {{ill|Sōja|en|Sōja|WD=Q873536|lt=Sōja|1=Sōja}}, indicating a potential connection between the two sites. Ōbosan Kofun was designated a Historic Site of Okayama Prefecture in 1959. Today, it is part of the Ōbo-no-Oka Historic Park and is open to the public. == Burial Facility == The burial facility is presumed to have been a horizontal stone chamber. However, the chamber was destroyed during the late Meiji period for stone quarrying, and only the sarcophagus remains. The sarcophagus, crafted from shell limestone sourced from {{ill|Ibara|en|Ibara|WD=Q863754|lt=Ibara, Okayama|1=Ibara}}, is a constructed house-shaped type comprising seven stone slabs. Its dimensions are as follows: * Sarcophagus body: 2.05 meters in length * Sarcophagus material: Shell limestone (Namakata stone) == Artifacts == Grave goods unearthed from the site include: * A bronze mirror (decorated with Buddhist and animal motifs) * Gilded bronze horse trappings * Iron weapons * Ornaments * Pottery (Sue pottery) These artifacts are currently housed in the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|WD=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum|1=Tokyo National Museum}}. However, it is suggested that some of the items may originate from neighboring Kofuns within the cluster. File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 出土 画文帯仏獣鏡.JPG|Bronze mirror
{{small|Exhibited at Tokyo National Museum}} File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 竪矧広板鋲留衝角付冑.JPG|Iron helmet with rivets
{{small|Tokyo National Museum}} File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 出土 鐘形杏葉.JPG|Bell-shaped horse gear
{{small|Tokyo National Museum}} File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 広口壺 (J-7646).JPG|Sue pottery jar
{{small|Tokyo National Museum}} File:王墓山古墳 (倉敷市) 出土 須恵器 蓋付・須恵器 高台付椀.JPG|Sue pottery lidded vessels and high-footed bowls
{{small|Tokyo National Museum}} == Cultural Properties == === Okayama Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Ōbosan Kofun - Designated on March 27, 1959[http://www.pref.okayama.jp/cgi-bin/bunka/culture/controller/client/resultDetail.cgi?id=336 Ōbosan Kofun] (Okayama Prefecture “Cultural Properties of Okayama”).. == Nearby Sites == * {{ill|Tatetsuki Site|ja|楯築墳丘墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatetsuki Site|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 楯築遺跡|1=Tatetsuki Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541408}} - One of the largest {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi|1=Yayoi period}} Kofuns in Japan. * Nihata Temple Site - Ruins of an ancient temple. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Explanatory panel (Kurashiki City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1974|chapter=Ōbosan Kofun (Akai Nishi No.1)|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/13111|title=Ōbosan Archaeological Sites|publisher=Kurashiki City Board of Education|pages=187–198}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1988|chapter=Ōbosan Kofun|title=Okayama Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japanese Historical Place names Encyclopedia]] Vol. 34|publisher=Heibonsha}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Obosan Kofun (Kurashiki)}} * [http://www.pref.okayama.jp/cgi-bin/bunka/culture/controller/client/resultDetail.cgi?id=336 Ōbosan Kofun] - Okayama Prefecture “Cultural Properties of Okayama” * [http://www.city.kurashiki.okayama.jp/5510.htm Ōbosan Kofun] - Kurashiki City Official Site {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|王墓山古墳|version=76800831|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q97342071|ja|王墓山古墳}} [[Category:History of Kurashiki]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
